perturbation.original_text: 3. The Receiving Party shall preserve Proprietary Information received from the Disclosing Party in confidence by exercising at least the same degree of care used to restrict disclosure and use of the Receiving Party's own similar confidential information, but at least a reasonable degree of care, and shall refrain from disclosing such Propriety Information to its employees not involved in the Pilot Study, its consultants, representatives, or agents ( the "Affiliates") or to any third party without written authorization from the Disclosing Party. This Agreement shall be construed to bind and impose obligations upon any other divisions, subsidiaries, business units, and/or affiliated companies of the Receiving Party that receive access to the Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall ensure that all copies of Proprietary Information stored electronically on its server, tape backup or on the hard drives of computers used by its Affiliates in connection with the Pilot Study have been permanently deleted and Receiving Party shall certify such deletion in writing to the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party will ensure that all of its Affiliates sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement containing confidentiality restrictions at least as restrictive as those set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The Receiving Party shall preserve Proprietary Information received from the Disclosing Party in confidence by exercising at least the same degree of care used to restrict disclosure and use of the Receiving Party's own similar confidential information, but at least a reasonable degree of care, and shall refrain from disclosing such Propriety Information to its employees not involved in the Pilot Study, its consultants, representatives, or agents ( the "Affiliates") or to any third party without written authorization from the Disclosing Party. This Agreement shall only bind the specific divisions, subsidiaries, business units, and/or affiliated companies of the Receiving Party explicitly named in writing and attached as Exhibit A. The Receiving Party shall ensure that all copies of Proprietary Information stored electronically on its server, tape backup or on the hard drives of computers used by its Affiliates in connection with the Pilot Study have been permanently deleted and Receiving Party shall certify such deletion in writing to the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party will ensure that all of its Affiliates sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement containing confidentiality restrictions at least as restrictive as those set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text states that the agreement binds all divisions, subsidiaries, business units, and/or affiliated companies of the Receiving Party. The changed text limits this to only those explicitly named in Exhibit A. This change creates uncertainty as to which entities are bound by the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. The Receiving Party shall preserve Proprietary Information received from the Disclosing Party in confidence by exercising at least the same degree of care used to restrict disclosure and use of the Receiving Party's own similar confidential information, but at least a reasonable degree of care, and shall refrain from disclosing such Propriety Information to its employees not involved in the Pilot Study, its consultants, representatives, or agents ( the "Affiliates") or to any third party without written authorization from the Disclosing Party. This Agreement shall only bind the specific divisions, subsidiaries, business units, and/or affiliated companies of the Receiving Party explicitly named in writing and attached as Exhibit A. The Receiving Party shall ensure that all copies of Proprietary Information stored electronically on its server, tape backup or on the hard drives of computers used by its Affiliates in connection with the Pilot Study have been permanently deleted and Receiving Party shall certify such deletion in writing to the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party will ensure that all of its Affiliates sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement containing confidentiality restrictions at least as restrictive as those set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states the agreement binds *all* divisions, subsidiaries, business units and/or affiliated companies. The changed text limits this to only those *explicitly named* in Exhibit A. This is a direct contradiction because the first statement covers a broad, undefined group, while the second limits it to a specific subset, creating uncertainty and conflicting obligations. Since the Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, the contradiction is maintained.

perturbation.original_text: 10. This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other. Unless thus earlier terminated, this Agreement shall terminate upon completion of the Pilot Study. The confidentiality obligations under this Agreement will survive termination of this Agreement for a period of three (3) years.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other. Notwithstanding the foregoing, ABP may terminate this agreement with no notification to the other party if there are delays caused by the Pilot Site. Unless thus earlier terminated, this Agreement shall terminate upon completion of the Pilot Study. The confidentiality obligations under this Agreement will survive termination of this Agreement for a period of three (3) years.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction. Originally, termination required thirty days' notice from either party. The addition allows ABP to terminate immediately under specific conditions, contradicting the initial 30-day notice requirement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other. Notwithstanding the foregoing, ABP may terminate this agreement with no notification to the other party if there are delays caused by the Pilot Site. Unless thus earlier terminated, this Agreement shall terminate upon completion of the Pilot Study. The confidentiality obligations under this Agreement will survive termination of this Agreement for a period of three (3) years.
perturbation.justification: The modified text allows ABP to terminate the agreement with no notification under certain circumstances, directly contradicting the original requirement of thirty days' written notice for termination by either party. The contradicted text is actually the same as the changed text, which confirms the contradiction exists within the same section. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Upon termination, the Receiving Party shall cease use of the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information, and shall destroy all Proprietary Information, including without limitation, reports, analyses, e-mails or any other derivative embodiments of the Proprietary Information, , together with all copies thereof, in its possession or control and furnish the Disclosing Party with written certification of destruction. Alternatively, at the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall return all such Proprietary Information, including without limitation, reports, analyses, e-mails or any other derivative embodiments of the Proprietary Information, together with all copies thereof to the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall ensure that all copies of Proprietary Information stored electronically on its server, tape backup or on the hard drives of computers used by Receiving Party's Affiliates in connection with the Pilot Study have been permanently deleted and Receiving Party shall certify such deletion in writing to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Upon termination, the Receiving Party shall cease use of the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information, and shall destroy all Proprietary Information, including without limitation, reports, analyses, e-mails or any other derivative embodiments of the Proprietary Information, , together with all copies thereof, in its possession or control and furnish the Disclosing Party with written certification of destruction. Notwithstanding the foregoing, ABP may retain one archival copy of the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information for legal compliance purposes only. Alternatively, at the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall return all such Proprietary Information, including without limitation, reports, analyses, e-mails or any other derivative embodiments of the Proprietary Information, together with all copies thereof to the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall ensure that all copies of Proprietary Information stored electronically on its server, tape backup or on the hard drives of computers used by Receiving Party's Affiliates in connection with the Pilot Study have been permanently deleted and Receiving Party shall certify such deletion in writing to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence "Notwithstanding the foregoing, ABP may retain one archival copy of the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information for legal compliance purposes only" creates a contradiction. The first sentence mandates the destruction of all proprietary information. This exception allows retention of a copy, creating ambiguity and potential disputes over compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Upon termination, the Receiving Party shall cease use of the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information, and shall destroy all Proprietary Information, including without limitation, reports, analyses, e-mails or any other derivative embodiments of the Proprietary Information, , together with all copies thereof, in its possession or control and furnish the Disclosing Party with written certification of destruction. Notwithstanding the foregoing, ABP may retain one archival copy of the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information for legal compliance purposes only. Alternatively, at the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall return all such Proprietary Information, including without limitation, reports, analyses, e-mails or any other derivative embodiments of the Proprietary Information, together with all copies thereof to the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall ensure that all copies of Proprietary Information stored electronically on its server, tape backup or on the hard drives of computers used by Receiving Party's Affiliates in connection with the Pilot Study have been permanently deleted and Receiving Party shall certify such deletion in writing to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the sentence "Notwithstanding the foregoing, ABP may retain one archival copy of the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information for legal compliance purposes only." This directly contradicts the initial requirement in the same section (and thus the contradicted text, which is the same section) that the Receiving Party "shall destroy all Proprietary Information". While the original text mandates complete destruction, the changed text allows for retention of one copy, creating a clear inconsistency regarding the Receiving Party's obligations upon termination, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as described in the explanation and aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Each party shall bear all costs and expenses incurred by it under or in connection with this Agreement, provided, however, that the prevailing party in any litigation commenced to enforce or construe the terms of this Agreement shall be entitled to collect from the other party the costs of such litigation, including reasonable attorneys' fees. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as an obligation by either party to enter a contract, subcontract, or other business relationship with the other party in connection with the Pilot Study.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Each party shall bear all costs and expenses incurred by it under or in connection with this Agreement, provided, however, that the prevailing party in any litigation commenced to enforce or construe the terms of this Agreement shall not be entitled to collect from the other party the costs of such litigation, including reasonable attorneys' fees, unless such claim is directly associated with proprietary rights. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as an obligation by either party to enter a contract, subcontract, or other business relationship with the other party in connection with the Pilot Study.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase "shall not be entitled to collect from the other party the costs of such litigation, including reasonable attorneys' fees, unless such claim is directly associated with proprietary rights" is directly contradictory to the original text that states "shall be entitled to collect from the other party the costs of such litigation, including reasonable attorneys' fees."
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. Each party shall bear all costs and expenses incurred by it under or in connection with this Agreement, provided, however, that the prevailing party in any litigation commenced to enforce or construe the terms of this Agreement shall not be entitled to collect from the other party the costs of such litigation, including reasonable attorneys' fees, unless such claim is directly associated with proprietary rights. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as an obligation by either party to enter a contract, subcontract, or other business relationship with the other party in connection with the Pilot Study.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 12 directly contradicts itself. The original text states that the prevailing party 'shall be entitled to collect...attorneys' fees.' The changed text modifies this to state they 'shall not be entitled to collect...attorneys' fees, unless such claim is directly associated with proprietary rights.' This is a direct contradiction within the same section of the agreement, which is also the contradicted section, making it unclear whether attorneys' fees can be collected by the prevailing party, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. The Receiving Party may disclose the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information to the extent required to be disclosed pursuant to final court order; provided, however, that the Receiving Party: (i) immediately notifies the Disclosing Party upon its receipt of any court order or other document that requests or demands disclosure of Proprietary Information; and (ii) assert the privileged and confidential nature of the Proprietary Information against the third party seeking disclosure; and (iii) cooperate fully with the Disclosing Party in protecting against any such disclosure and/or obtaining a protective order narrowing the scope of such disclosure of the Proprietary Information. In the event that such protection against disclosure is not obtained, the receiving party will be entitled to disclose the Proprietary Information, but only to the extent necessary to legally comply with such compelled disclosure and to have such required disclosure made under a protective secrecy order.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. The Receiving Party may disclose the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information to the extent required to be disclosed pursuant to final court order; provided, however, that the Receiving Party: (i) immediately notifies the Disclosing Party upon its receipt of any court order or other document that requests or demands disclosure of Proprietary Information; and (ii) assert the privileged and confidential nature of the Proprietary Information against the third party seeking disclosure; and (iii) cooperate fully with the Disclosing Party in protecting against any such disclosure and/or obtaining a protective order narrowing the scope of such disclosure of the Proprietary Information. In the event that such protection against disclosure is not obtained, the receiving party may disclose the Proprietary Information regardless of extent necessary to legally comply with such compelled disclosure and to have such required disclosure made without a protective secrecy order.
perturbation.explanation: The change, removing the clause 'but only to the extent necessary to legally comply with such compelled disclosure and to have such required disclosure made under a protective secrecy order.' contradicts the beginning sentence, that states Receiving Party may disclose only the amount the court needs, while change allows it to disclose all Proprietary Information regardless.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. The Receiving Party may disclose the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information to the extent required to be disclosed pursuant to final court order; provided, however, that the Receiving Party: (i) immediately notifies the Disclosing Party upon its receipt of any court order or other document that requests or demands disclosure of Proprietary Information; and (ii) assert the privileged and confidential nature of the Proprietary Information against the third party seeking disclosure; and (iii) cooperate fully with the Disclosing Party in protecting against any such disclosure and/or obtaining a protective order narrowing the scope of such disclosure of the Proprietary Information. In the event that such protection against disclosure is not obtained, the receiving party may disclose the Proprietary Information regardless of extent necessary to legally comply with such compelled disclosure and to have such required disclosure made without a protective secrecy order.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the limitation on disclosure, allowing the Receiving Party to disclose all Proprietary Information regardless of what the court needs. This directly contradicts the initial sentence of both the original text and the contradicted text, which states that disclosure should only be 'to the extent required to be disclosed pursuant to final court order'. The removal of 'but only to the extent necessary to legally comply with such compelled disclosure and to have such required disclosure made under a protective secrecy order' creates an inconsistency that makes the two clauses incompatible, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
